quotes.text: You want the truth? The truth doesn't care what you want.
quotes.speaker: A Few Good Men (1992)
quotes.context: Col. Jessup during the courtroom confrontation
quotes.fake_note: Eerily close to Jessup's actual blustering courtroom monologue style. Subtle giveaway: Jessup's real lines are more self-aggrandizing; this is slightly too philosophical and detached.

quotes.text: Every scar you carry is a map of somewhere you survived.
quotes.speaker: Rocky (1976)
quotes.context: Mickey to Rocky in the gym
quotes.fake_note: Matches Mickey's gruff-but-poetic trainer wisdom and the film's working-class grit. Subtle giveaway: slightly too lyrical; Mickey's real dialogue is more blunt and specific.

quotes.speaker: Apollo 13 (1995)
quotes.context: Aboard the damaged spacecraft, the crew reports the malfunction to mission control. The line is spoken by Jim Lovell (Tom Hanks), echoing Jack Swigert; the film's "we have a problem" differs from the real transmission's "we've had a problem."

quotes.text: I'm kind of a big deal.
quotes.speaker: Anchorman: The Legend of Ron Burgundy (2004)
quotes.context: Ron Burgundy (Will Ferrell) boasting to Veronica Corningstone about his fame

quotes.text: I've seen what hope does to a man. It's crueler than despair.
quotes.speaker: The Shawshank Redemption (1994)
quotes.context: Red in voiceover, reflecting on prison life
quotes.fake_note: Perfectly captures Red's weary, Morgan Freeman voiceover cadence and the film's themes. Subtle giveaway: the film ultimately argues the opposite — this inverts the message just enough.
